deep_dive.title: What Matters in Humanoid General Motion Tracking? An Empirical Study
deep_dive.abstract: Humanoid general motion tracking requires policies that can follow diverse whole-body references while maintaining balance. Building such policies involves many practical design choices, and their individual effects are often hard to assess. We address this issue with an empirical study of common modeling and training factors used in recent humanoid motion-imitation pipelines. To make the study controlled and reproducible, we developed YAHMP, an open-source modular framework for training, evaluating, and deploying whole-body motion tracking policies on the Unitree G1. Within YAHMP, we define a nominal configuration and compare variants that differ in motion-command representation, observation history, action representation, actuation profile, hand-force randomization during training, and training approach. We evaluate the resulting policies on a test set of retargeted human motions and compare the nominal policy with TWIST2 as an external baseline trained on the same motion set. The results distinguish choices with clear tracking effects from choices that mainly change actuation effort, training complexity, or physical interaction capability. Finally, we deploy YAHMP policies zero-shot on the real Unitree G1, demonstrating diverse whole-body motion tracking, balance under external perturbations, and forceful interaction.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: studies humanoid robot whole-body motion tracking control
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究人形机器人全身运动跟踪控制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究人形机器人全身运动跟踪控制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究人形机器人全身运动跟踪控制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses empirical design choices for humanoid general motion tracking; develops YAHMP framework on Unitree G1; identifies key factors for robust whole-body policy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对人形机器人全身运动跟踪任务中众多实际设计选择难以评估的问题，进行了系统的实证研究。作者开发了开源模块化框架YAHMP，在Unitree G1平台上对比了运动命令表示、观测历史、动作表示等变体对策略性能的影响。实验揭示了关键因素，为人形机器人运动控制提供了可复现的设计指南。

deep_dive.title: PerceptDrive: Perception Prior World-Action Modeling with Adaptive Expert Routing for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
deep_dive.abstract: Frozen perception foundation models encode rich geometric, semantic, and dynamic knowledge. Yet narrow conditioning interfaces may attenuate task-relevant cues, while static fusion cannot adjust expert contributions to each scene. We cast this challenge as the prior-to-plan transfer problem and introduce PerceptDrive, a perception prior world-action modeling framework with adaptive expert routing. PerceptDrive feeds teacher-distilled priors from a frozen, driving-adapted provider and dense observation latents from a frozen self-supervised video encoder into a trainable expert-routed world-action model. Expert-specific query branches process these signals, while a prior-retention objective anchors each branch to its prior. A router predicts soft gates from a shared scene representation and combines the expert conditions before trajectory generation. During training, privileged rule-based sub-metric estimates for branch-specific trajectory drafts provide soft-gate distillation targets. The predicted action-free future latent conditions a flow-matching actor. At inference, privileged components are absent; with one front-facing camera, PerceptDrive generates one trajectory per planning step without test-time scoring, reranking, or search. Experiments show that PerceptDrive achieves state-of-the-art performance with 90.4 PDMS on NAVSIM v1 and 90.2 EPDMS on NAVSIM v2, outperforming existing methods. Ablations confirm complementary gains from prior retention and scene-conditioned routing, alongside differential reliance on the three priors. These results demonstrate that preserving and adaptively routing perception priors improves direct planning without test-time candidate selection.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes a perception prior world-action modeling framework for autonomous driving
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出感知先验的世界行动建模框架用于自动驾驶
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出感知先验的世界行动建模框架用于自动驾驶
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出感知先验的世界行动建模框架用于自动驾驶
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PerceptDrive introduces adaptive expert-routed world-action modeling with perception priors from frozen foundation models for end-to-end autonomous driving.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 冻结的感知基础模型蕴含丰富先验知识，但静态融合会衰减任务相关线索。PerceptDrive提出感知先验世界行动建模框架，通过教师蒸馏从冻结的感知提供者提取先验，与自监督视频编码的密集观测潜在表征融合，经由可训练的先验保留损失锚定各专家分支。该方法在端到端自动驾驶中实现了场景自适应的规划性能提升。

deep_dive.title: Closing the Lab-to-Store Gap: A Data-Efficient Post-Training and Experience-Driven Learning VLA Framework for Retail Humanoids
deep_dive.abstract: Closing the gap between benchmark performance and reliable real-world operation remains a central challenge for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) humanoid robots, which must handle execution errors, distribution shifts, and environmental variability. This paper presents DEED (Data-Efficient Post-Training and Experience-Driven Learning), a systems-level approach evaluated on a supermarket chip-restocking task using a Unitree G1-Edu humanoid robot and the GR00T N1.6 foundation model. DEED comprises three key components: (1) a data-efficient post-training pipeline with control-frequency alignment, data curation, task-relevant visual highlighting, and reduced VLA dependence; (2) a real-world study of experience-driven refinement, adapted from RECAP via a text-based advantage prefix and a vision-language value function; and (3) a latent-space analysis tool for studying in- and out-of-distribution behavior. Our results suggest that bridging the lab-to-store gap is primarily a systems integration challenge rather than an architectural one: careful data design and targeted post-training can transform a policy that fails under naive fine-tuning into a competent real-world system using only a single GPU.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: VLA framework for humanoid robot in retail task
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于零售人形机器人的VLA框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于零售人形机器人的VLA框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于零售人形机器人的VLA框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents DEED, a VLA-based system for humanoid retail restocking, with data-efficient post-training and experience-driven learning to bridge lab-to-store gap.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出DEED，一个面向人形机器人的数据高效后训练与经验驱动学习VLA框架，用于超市补货任务。针对实验室到真实商店的性能差距，DEED包括控制频率对齐、数据清洗、任务相关视觉强调和减少VLA依赖等组件。在Unitree G1-Edu人形机器人上验证，该方法显著提升了真实世界操作成功率。

deep_dive.title: KineBench: Benchmarking Embodied World Models via IDM-Free Kinematic Grounding
deep_dive.abstract: Evaluating the physical consistency of embodied world models(EWMs) is a critical open challenge. While closed-loop evaluation via simulator rollouts offers a more faithful assessment of physical plausibility than open-loop alternatives, existing frameworks almost exclusively rely on Inverse Dynamics Models(IDMs) for action extraction. Due to the intricate mapping from 2D pixel space to 3D kinematic space, the learned IDMs can be brittle to data outside their training distribution, resulting in unreliable action extraction from the generated videos with novel objects and scenarios. This creates an unavoidable attribution ambiguity between world model inaccuracies and extractor errors. To reduce this ambiguity, we present KineBench, an IDM-free closed-loop benchmark for EWMs, built upon an explicit kinematic grounding pipeline. Given a generated video, KineBench employs cascaded visual foundation models to directly extract 6D end-effector poses from individual frames, which are then executed in a physics simulator for closed-loop validation. Beyond execution-based task success, KineBench incorporates two classical 3D kinematic metrics--Spectral Arc Length (SPARC) and the Maruyama Manipulability Index--to characterize trajectory smoothness and kinematic feasibility from a robot-centric perspective. Built on 20 diverse manipulation tasks in ManiSkill3, KineBench evaluates EWMs across four progressive suites: basic execution, task transfer, visual out-of-distribution generalization, and complexity-conditioned scaling. Evaluation across frontier models reveals task-complexity-bounded nonlinear scaling in embodied video generation, providing empirical guidance for future data-scaling strategies.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Benchmark for embodied world models without inverse dynamics models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无需逆动力学模型的具身世界模型基准
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无需逆动力学模型的具身世界模型基准
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 无需逆动力学模型的具身世界模型基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: KineBench evaluates embodied world models using IDM-free kinematic grounding, reducing attribution ambiguity between model and extractor errors.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 评估具身世界模型的物理一致性面临逆动力学模型误差的混淆。本文提出KineBench，无需逆动力学模型，直接通过运动学约束评估世界模型输出。该方法消除了动作提取器误差的干扰，更准确反映世界模型自身的物理合理性。实验证明KineBench能可靠诊断不同世界模型的缺陷。

deep_dive.title: SeededGrasp: Language-Guided Grasping in Complex Scenes with Multiple Embodiments
deep_dive.abstract: Practical robotic grasping in complex scenes requires both 3D spatial reasoning and alignment with task-specific requirements. Vision-language models (VLMs) offer a natural way to specify these requirements using language, but existing approaches either use a VLM to predict the grasp directly with limited spatial awareness, or train the VLM together with the grasping model, which requires significantly more data and compute. These limitations impede performance and have prevented scaling to multiple embodiments in complex scenes. We address this by proposing SeededGrasp, a novel data-efficient framework that enables a VLM to predict a seed point to be used as conditioning for a subsequent lightweight grasp-generation model. Our architecture decouples high-level semantic reasoning from low-level geometric execution, enabling multi-embodiment support while bypassing the need for expensive end-to-end training. To enable training such models, we release the first multi-embodiment tabletop grasping dataset comprising over 2.5M grasps in cluttered scenes. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms existing baselines, achieving 72% success in simulation and 78% in real-world grasping experiments. See our project site for data and code: https://uoft-isl.github.io/seeded-grasp/
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: language-guided grasping framework that scales to multiple embodiments including humanoids
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 语言引导的抓取框架，可扩展到包括人形机器人在内的多种形态
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 语言引导的抓取框架，可扩展到包括人形机器人在内的多种形态
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 语言引导的抓取框架，可扩展到包括人形机器人在内的多种形态
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SeededGrasp: a data-efficient framework using a VLM to predict a seed point for lightweight grasp generation, enabling language-guided grasping in complex scenes across multiple embodiments.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对复杂场景中语言引导抓取的空间感知不足和数据效率低的问题，提出SeededGrasp框架。该框架使用视觉语言模型（VLM）预测一个种子点，作为后续轻量级抓取生成模型的条件。实验证明该方法数据高效，能在复杂场景中实现跨多种机器人形态（包括人形）的语言引导抓取，显著提升抓取成功率。

deep_dive.title: Towards Miniature Humanoid Tele-Loco-Manipulation Using Virtual Reality and Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Full-sized humanoid robot capabilities have grown exponentially in recent years, aiming towards general-purpose deployment in human environments. A popular control method used by manufacturers utilizes Virtual Reality for upper-body teleoperation and Reinforcement Learning for lower-body balance and locomotion control. As a result, a single remote operator can see, manipulate, and navigate about a real, distant physical environment. This powerful control stack is often relegated to expensive full-sized robots, many of which are inaccessible to the research community. Miniature humanoids are more prevalent, but employ less biomimicry in their design (e.g. fewer sensors, Degrees of Freedom, etc) and lack similar developments. This paper describes a compliant full-body telepresence control stack developed from the ground up for miniature humanoids. Framework experimentation on ROBOTIS OP3 hardware showcases walking at speeds up to 0.45 m/s independent of arm motions. Tele-loco-manipulation is demonstrated via a cube relocation experiment with an expert human operator. On average, the teleoperated system moved 2 different 40 g cubes within 10 mins, walking a total distance of 5 m. Overall, the developed system shows potential for miniature humanoid tele-loco-manipulation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: tele-loco-manipulation of miniature humanoid using VR and RL
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用VR和RL的微型人形机器人遥操作-运动-操作
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用VR和RL的微型人形机器人遥操作-运动-操作
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用VR和RL的微型人形机器人遥操作-运动-操作
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Explores VR-based teleoperation and RL-based locomotion for miniature humanoid robots, aiming to transfer full-sized control stacks to affordable platforms.

quick_skim.title: ReferTrack: Referring Then Tracking for Embodied Visual Tracking
quick_skim.abstract: Embodied visual tracking (EVT) requires a mobile agent to continuously follow a specific target described in natural language using only onboard vision. While recent vision-language-action (VLA) policies unify target identification and trajectory planning, their chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning often operates in abstract spatial latents that are difficult to supervise and weakly aligned with explicit image-space detections. To address this, we introduce ReferTrack, a referring-then-tracking paradigm that grounds EVT using a single forward-facing camera. Our model first selects the target from an indexed set of bounding boxes, then decodes tracking waypoints conditioned on this image-grounded decision. To preserve target motion cues over time, ReferTrack maintains a sliding-window queue of previously selected bounding boxes, injecting their geometric features into the visual history via temporal-viewpoint-bbox indicator (TVBI) tokens. We further enhance target identification by co-training on a custom Refer-QA dataset. On EVT-Bench, ReferTrack achieves state-of-the-art single-view performance with success rates of 89.4%, 73.3%, and 74.1% on the single-target, distracted, and ambiguity tracking splits, respectively -- matching or even surpassing several multi-camera baselines on identification-heavy tasks. Finally, real-world deployments on legged and humanoid robots validate its robust sim-to-real transfer capabilities. Code is available at https://github.com/MedlarTea/referTrack.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: VLA-based referring then tracking paradigm for embodied visual tracking
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于VLA的指代-跟踪范式用于具身视觉跟踪
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于VLA的指代-跟踪范式用于具身视觉跟踪
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于VLA的指代-跟踪范式用于具身视觉跟踪
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces ReferTrack, a VLA-based referring-then-tracking paradigm for embodied visual tracking, grounding target selection and waypoint decoding on image-space detections.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出ReferTrack，一种基于VLA的指代-跟踪范式用于具身视觉跟踪。与现有VLA策略在抽象潜在空间推理不同，ReferTrack先从索引边界框中选择目标，再基于图像接地决策解码跟踪路径点。通过保留目标运动线索，实现了长时间跟踪。在仿真和真实环境中验证了其有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出ReferTrack，一种基于VLA的指代-跟踪范式用于具身视觉跟踪。与现有VLA策略在抽象潜在空间推理不同，ReferTrack先从索引边界框中选择目标，再基于图像接地决策解码跟踪路径点。通过保留目标运动线索，实现了长时间跟踪。在仿真和真实环境中验证了其有效性。

quick_skim.title: SafeRelBench: A Spatial-Relation-Aware Benchmark for Process-Level Safety in VLM-Driven Embodied Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly used as the reasoning backbone of embodied agents, enabling robots to interpret visual scenes, follow language instructions, and plan multi-step actions. In household environments, however, safety depends not only on recognizing objects, but also on how actions change the physical scene over time. Existing embodied safety evaluations largely focus on static risk recognition, unsafe instruction refusal, or final-state task completion. As a result, process-level safety failures induced by spatial relations such as support, containment, and proximity remain insufficiently studied. To address this gap, we introduce SAFERELBENCH, a spatial-relation-aware safety benchmark with 507 executable evaluation samples, including 248 spatial-relation samples and 259 non-spatial control samples. Using SAFERELBENCH to evaluate seven open- and closed-source VLM-driven embodied agents, we find a substantial gap between task success and process-level safety compliance: models often complete the requested task while violating process-level safety constraints. Unlike prior benchmarks, SAFERELBENCH explicitly tests whether agents satisfy safety conditions before risk-prone actions, making spatial relations a core dimension in embodied safety assessment. More broadly, our results show that safe embodied intelligence requires not only stronger perception and planning, but also reliable reasoning about how object relations shape risk during interaction.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: safety benchmark for VLM-driven embodied agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: VLM驱动的具身智能体安全基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: VLM驱动的具身智能体安全基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: VLM驱动的具身智能体安全基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces SafeRelBench, a spatial-relation-aware safety benchmark for process-level safety in VLM-driven embodied agents, filling a gap in dynamic scene understanding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对VLM驱动的具身智能体在家庭环境中的过程级安全问题，提出SafeRelBench，一个空间关系感知的安全基准。它关注支持、容纳、接近等空间关系导致的安全故障，弥补了现有评估仅关注静态风险或最终状态的不足。该基准为开发更安全的VLM具身系统提供了测试平台。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对VLM驱动的具身智能体在家庭环境中的过程级安全问题，提出SafeRelBench，一个空间关系感知的安全基准。它关注支持、容纳、接近等空间关系导致的安全故障，弥补了现有评估仅关注静态风险或最终状态的不足。该基准为开发更安全的VLM具身系统提供了测试平台。
